title_original: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence (Artificial Intelligence Act)
issuing_body: European Parliament and Council of the European Union

key_dates.event: published in the Official Journal (OJ L, 2024/1689)
prohibitions (Art 5) and AI-literacy obligations began to apply

Council final approval of the digital-omnibus amendment deferring high-risk obligations (Annex III to 2027-12-02; Annex I-embedded to 2028-08-02); amending act pending OJ publication as of last_verified — Art 50(1) transparency NOT deferred
Art 50 transparency obligations apply (unchanged by omnibus for 50(1); 50(2) watermarking deferred to 2026-12-02 for systems already on market)

summary_md: ■ FACT: Adopted 2024, in force since 2024-08-01, applying in stages; classified enacted_law per SPEC §9.3 (primary legislation despite the EU-law label 'Regulation'). ■ FACT: A June 2026 omnibus amendment — finally approved by the Council on 2026-06-29 and pending Official Journal publication as of 2026-07-12 — defers the Annex III high-risk obligations to 2027-12-02 and Annex I-embedded to 2028-08-02, while leaving Art 50(1) transparency applying from 2026-08-02. ▲ INFERENCE (medium): until the amending act is published, the dates on the books remain those of Reg 2024/1689; records here therefore carry the original applies_from with the deferral documented in key_dates.
analyst_notes: Pass 1 (authoring) 2026-07-12. Consilium press-release URL returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch (known bot-blocking host, MJ-7 allowlist); existence and content corroborated via multiple independent Tier-3 law-firm alerts and the Parliament endorsement of 2026-06-16. Re-verify OJ publication of the amending act before Pass 2 review; when published, update key_dates with the new CELEX and re-score any affected assessments (§19.5).

issuing_body: European Parliament and Council of the European Union

key_dates.event: published in the Official Journal (OJ L 119)

summary_md: ■ FACT: Fully applicable since 2018-05-25; the EU's horizontal data-protection regime. Amendment history does not change its live status (amendment = event, not status — SPEC §8/MJ-4). ▲ INFERENCE (medium): Art 22's automated-decision-making right is the primary GDPR hook for agentic systems making consequential decisions (C8).
analyst_notes: Pass 1 (authoring) 2026-07-12. Instrument metadata verified against EUR-Lex CELEX record this session; Art 22 verbatim wording not re-fetched this session (paraphrase-only provision, confidence capped accordingly).
